input.prompt:
After upgrading to MSTest 4.x, my tests that use Assert.IsInstanceOfType with
an out parameter no longer compile. I get errors like:
- CS1615: Argument 2 should not be passed with the 'out' keyword
How do I update these assertions?

data.reasoningText:
The user is on MSTest 3.8.0 and wants to upgrade to MSTest 4.x. In MSTest 3.x, `Assert.IsInstanceOfType<T>(object, out T)` had an `out` parameter. In MSTest 4.x, this signature changed — the generic overload now returns the typed value directly instead of using an `out` parameter.

In MSTest 4.x, the signature is:
```csharp
T Assert.IsInstanceOfType<T>(object value);
```

So instead of:
```csharp
Assert.IsInstanceOfType<FastProcessor>(processor, out var typed);


```

I can now call it directly and assign the result to a variable. I need to update the test file and the project file to reflect this change.

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args: {"path": "/tmp/sv-4f1f7c3d39004178a8d9fe557cf8a586/TypeCheckTests.cs", "old_str": "        Assert.IsInstanceOfType<FastProcessor>(processor, out var typed);\n        Assert.AreEqual(\"Fast\", typed.Name);", "new_str": "        var typed = Assert.IsInstanceOfType<FastProcessor>(processor);\n        Assert.AreEqual(\"Fast\", typed.Name);"}

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args: {"path": "/tmp/sv-4f1f7c3d39004178a8d9fe557cf8a586/TypeCheckTests.cs", "old_str": "        Assert.IsInstanceOfType<SlowProcessor>(processor, out var typed);\n        Assert.AreEqual(\"Slow\", typed.Name);", "new_str": "        var typed = Assert.IsInstanceOfType<SlowProcessor>(processor);\n        Assert.AreEqual(\"Slow\", typed.Name);"}

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args: {"path": "/tmp/sv-4f1f7c3d39004178a8d9fe557cf8a586/TypeCheckTests.cs", "old_str": "        Assert.IsInstanceOfType<IProcessor>(processor, out var typed);\n        Assert.IsNotNull(typed.Name);", "new_str": "        var typed = Assert.IsInstanceOfType<IProcessor>(processor);\n        Assert.IsNotNull(typed.Name);"}
